data.title: Meet Aptera Motors: The solar-powered electric vehicle company that just went public
data.description:
Aptera Motors Corp. (NASDAQ:SEV) is going public on Thursday through a direct listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market. Unlike a traditional IPO, Aptera's (NASDAQ:SEV) listing does not raise new capital for the company. Instead, the direct listing allows existing shareholders to sell their shares on the open market.

"Listing on Nasdaq represents a pivotal chapter in our journey and is a testament to the dedication of our team and the passion of our global community," highlighted Co-CEO Chris Anthony. “We believe this step will help us accelerate our mission to deliver a solar-powered future and support the continued development of our first production vehicle for our reservation holders.” 

The solar mobility company registered approximately 31.7 million Class B common shares for resale by existing holders. The listing has no underwriters, with Northland Securities serving as financial advisor.

Aptera Motors (SEV) described its flagship vehicle as a paradigm-shifting solar electric vehicle that leverages breakthroughs in aerodynamics, material science, and solar technology to pursue new levels of efficiency. As a public benefit corporation, Aptera Motors (SEV) noted that it is committed to building a sustainable business that positively impacts its stakeholders and the environment. 

 

 

 

Aptera Motors (SEV) was founded originally in 2006. The company went bankrupt in 2011 after failing to secure a Department of Energy loan and sufficient private investment but was reborn in 2019 with a renewed emphasis on solar-powered electric vehicles. 

In terms of financials, Aptera (SEV) reported a net loss of $22.9 million for the first half of 2025, compared to a $17.8 million loss for the same period in 2024. The company had $13.1 million in cash and cash equivalents at the end of June, down from $18.1 million at the end of June in 2024. Notably, Aptera (SEV) recently announced a $75 million equity line of credit with New Circle Capital to fund production tooling and readiness. The financing can be tapped incrementally based on market conditions and strategic milestones.

data.title: No AI bubble here: VanEck highlights real cash-flow backing expansion
data.description:
VanEck dismissed concerns about an artificial intelligence (AI) bubble, emphasizing that the current surge in AI adoption is fundamentally different from the speculative frenzy of the late 1990s dot-com era.

According to the exchange traded fund provider, today’s AI expansion is underpinned by tangible profits and significant infrastructure commitments rather than hype-driven speculation. 

During the dot-com boom, the investment institution said startups raced for growth with limited revenue and easy access to venture capital, often without sustainable business models. In contrast, the AI wave is being powered by some of the world’s most profitable technology companies, which are reinvesting substantial free cash flow into long-term infrastructure projects essential to maintaining competitiveness in the AI era. 

“Artificial intelligence is driving one of the most significant infrastructure build-outs in modern history. Unlike the dot-com boom of the late 1990s, today’s AI expansion is being led by profitable global companies deploying existing cash flow,” VanEck said. 

VanEck highlighted that Amazon (AMZN) expects roughly $100 billion in capital expenditures for 2025, much of it focused on cloud computing and AI. Microsoft (MSFT) plans around $80 billion, Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL) has increased its target to $85 billion, and Meta (META) anticipates spending between $66 billion and $72 billion. These allocations represent deliberate balance-sheet decisions, not speculative gambles. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (NYSEARCA:DDM), (NYSEARCA:DOG), (NYSEARCA:DXD), (NYSEARCA:SDOW), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), (NYSEARCA:SPXU), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NASDAQ:QQQM), (NASDAQ:TQQQ), (NYSEARCA:QID), and (NASDAQ:SQQQ). 

Artificial Intelligence Focused ETFs: (NASDAQ:AIQ), (NASDAQ:BOTZ), (NYSEARCA:DTEC), (BATS:WTAI), (NASDAQ:XAIX), (NASDAQ:WISE), (NYSEARCA:GINN), (NASDAQ:ROBT), (NYSEARCA:TECB), (NASDAQ:XT), (NYSEARCA:THNQ), and (NYSEARCA:CHAT).

data.title: Citi trims estimates on Texas Instruments due to automotive exposure
data.description:
Citi Research analyzed analog semiconductor companies based on each one's exposure to various end markets, finding Texas Instruments (NASDAQ:TXN) to be under the most pressure due to its heavy exposure to a weak automotive end market. 

Citi lowered its target price on Texas Instruments to $235 from $250 and lowered its earnings per share for the upcoming fiscal year to $6.66 from $7.33.

"We are trimming estimates on Buy-rated Texas Instruments as the analog upturn has not begun as fast as we expected," said Citi analysts, led by Christopher Danely, in a Thursday research note. "We believe the tariff war is causing the supply chain to hold back on capacity and inventory and as a result, customers will hold off on building inventory until the last second."

Citi considers auto as the weakest end market and industrial as the strongest. The auto market accounts for 30% of Texas Instruments sales, while industrial accounts for 36%. However, Microchip Technology (NASDAQ:MCHP) only receives 16% of its revenue from the auto sector and 48% from industrial.

Citi retained its Buy rating and $90 price target on Microchip Technology. However, they still lowered its upcoming fiscal year EPS estimate to $3.43 from $3.62.

"We are trimming estimates on Buy-rated Microchip as the analog upturn has not begun as fast as we expected," Danely noted. "We would note our F26 EPS estimate is still 38% above Consensus and we still expect Microchip to exhibit the strongest print/guide given its 60% exposure to the Industrial/Data Center end markets."

Microchip Technology and Texas Instruments were up 0.9% and 0.5%, respectively, during Thursday market trading.

data.title: ClearBridge Global Value Improvers Strategy adds Alphabet, exits Novo Nordisk in Q3
data.description:
• ClearBridge Global Value Improvers Strategy generated positive absolute returns in the third quarter, but trailed its MSCI World Value Index benchmark on the back of select holdings within industrials and energy.
  • The fund initiated new positions in Lloyds Banking Group (NYSE:LYG) (OTCPK:LLDTF) and Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) (TSX:GOOG:CA) during the quarter.
  • ClearBridge Global Value Improvers Strategy exited from Novo Nordisk (NVO) (OTCPK:NONOF) (NVON:CA), Coty (NYSE:COTY), PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia (OTCPK:BKRKY) (OTCPK:BKRKF), and Fiserv (NYSE:FI).
  • Fund Letter

data.title: Microsoft adds new features to AI Copilot in Windows 11
data.description:
• Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) on Thursday announced several new features for its artificial intelligence chatbot, Copilot, bringing more functionality to its ubiquitous Windows 11 operating system.
  • Windows 11 users can now use the wake words, “Hey, Copilot” to complete a task, Microsoft said in a blog post. The feature requires users to opt-in and gives them “a new way to easily start a conversation with Copilot Voice, anytime the feature is enabled.” CEO Satya Nadella described the new feature as a way to let users “talk naturally” to their computers.
  • The Nadella-led company also said it was expanding Copilot Vision, a feature that can analyze content on users' screens and answer questions related to that content, worldwide.
  • In addition, Microsoft said it would be launching new features for Windows 11 Insiders, adding agentic AI capabilities.
  • The company also provided on its Xbox-Windows gaming features, unveiling Gaming Copilot on the handheld ROG Xbox Ally and ROG Xbox Ally X devices.
  • Microsoft shares rose fractionally in morning trading.

data.title: Cidara Therapeutics gains overweight rating at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
• Morgan Stanley has initiated Cidara Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CDTX) at overweight, highlighting the promise of its lead candidate, CD388, a single–dose antiviral that could be a potential complement to vaccines for prevention of seasonal and pandemic influenza.
  • The investment firm set a $190 price target (~80% upside based on Oct. 15 close). 
  • CD388 is considered a drug-Fc conjugate as it combines the antiviral zanamivir, also known as GSK's (GSK) Relenza, with a human antibody fragment. It has FDA Fast Track and Breakthrough Designation statuses.
  • Analyst Maxwell Skor said that based on strong phase 2b results, he sees CD388 as providing a significant opportunity for influenza prophylaxis.  
  • "At this stage, we view CD388 as complementary to vaccination, with potential for additive 60-90%+ preventive efficacy, and see additional standalone use in poor vaccine responders and those who have an aversion to vaccination - expanding the initial US eligible pool from ~50M (comorbid and immunocompromised) to an additional >100M (generally healthy adults ≥65+)," Skor wrote.
  • He added that the candidate could address a $5B+ market opportunity of ~150M patients in the US.

data.title: Travelers stock dips as Q3 earnings reflect soft net premiums written
data.description:
The Travelers Companies (NYSE:TRV) shares slid 2.5% in Thursday morning trading after the insurance company's third-quarter results reflected below-consensus net premiums written. Overall, though, earnings topped the average analyst estimate, driven by "very strong underwriting results and higher investment income," Chairman and CEO Alan Schnitzer said.

Q3 core EPS of $8.14, surpassing the $6.32 expected, rose from $6.51 in Q2 and $5.24 a year ago.

Total revenues of $12.5B, exceeding the $11.2B consensus, advanced from $10.9B in the prior quarter and $11.9B in Q3 2024.

Net premiums written totaled $11.5B in Q3, virtually unchanged from Q2 and up from $11.3B in the year-earlier quarter. That also trails the $11.8B Visible Alpha estimate.

Net investment income increased to $1.03B from $942M in Q2 and $904M in last year's Q3.

Underlying combined ratio of 83.9% improved from 84.7% in Q2 and 85.6% in Q3 2024.

Pretax catastrophe losses, mainly resulting from severe wind and hail storms in multiple states, were $402M vs. $927M in Q2 and $939M in Q3 2024.

Pretax underlying underwriting income was $1.76B, up from $1.63B in the previous quarter and $1.50B in Q3 2024.

data.title: Salesforce surges following robust growth targets from Investor Day
data.description:
Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) shares jumped 7% during early market action on Thursday following ambitious growth targets outlined by the software company during its Investor Day presentation at Dreamforce 2025 on Wednesday.

Most financial analysts provided positive reactions following the event.

Cantor reiterated its Overweight rating and $325 price target on the stock.

"Management set ambitious growth targets to reach $60b of revenue in FY30, or a ~10% organic CAGR," said Cantor analysts Matthew VanVliet and Mason Marion in an investor note. "This compares to 8.8% guidance for FY26. Key drivers include continued multi-cloud penetration, particularly Data 360, Agentforce adoption and monetization, and pricing & packaging."

KeyBanc also retained its Overweight rating and $400 price target.

"Net-new AOV growing faster than overall AOV (average order value) mathematically leads to an acceleration," said KeyBanc analysts Jackson Ader and Jack Nichols in an investor note. "Management has said it aspired to double-digit growth in recent quarters but hadn't put a timeline on it until today. It's a big company and a barge of a top-line, but the inflection is now expected to come in 12–18 months. This leads to a target of $60B+ in FY30, an organic growth CAGR of over 10%."

Piper Sandler reiterated its Overweight rating and $315 price target.

"Net new average order value has emerged as primary metric internally aligning the entire company behind the goal of driving a durable top-line reacceleration," said Piper Sandler analysts Hannah Rudoff and J.R. Herrera in a Thursday investor note. 

"While NNAOV growth was largely suppressed from FY23-FY25 (growing less than AOV), bookings acceleration and retention improvements have driven a strong rebound with NNAOV growth beginning to exceed AOV growth, which has given management confidence in a growth inflection occurring within 12-18 months (could prove conservative)," Rudoff added.

However, RBC reiterated its Sector Perform rating and a modest $250 price target.

"Stepping back, while the medium-term targets are encouraging and ahead of our expectations, we remain on the sidelines," said RBC analysts, led by Rishi Jaluria, in a note. "This stance reflects uncertainty around long-term Agentforce adoption and monetization, as well as potential execution risk from Informatica."

data.title: U.S. stocks add to gains as more corporate earnings lift sentiment amid U.S. government shutdown
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages rose on Thursday as investors continued to assess earnings reports while the U.S. government shutdown entered its third week.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.4% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.7%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.2%.

On earnings, Charles Schwab (SCHW) shares rose +1% after earnings beat. U.S. Bancorp (USB) also reported earnings growth, with shares +1.5%

“The good news is that earnings are doing the heavy lifting as we enter year 4 of this bull market. Following Friday’s decline, the P/E ratio is only up 1% year-over-year, while earnings are up 11%. This is in line with my expectation at the beginning of the year that after two years of substantial P/E expansion, earnings would become the dominant driver of returns in 2025,” said Jurrien Timmer, director of global macro at Fidelity.

“If history is any guide, we should see a meaningful bounce from the 7% growth estimate. A good bounce seems likely following the sharp uptick in earnings revisions in recent months,” Timmer added.

In other tech news, Salesforce (CRM) +7.2% and Google (GOOGL) (GOOG) announced an expansion of their existing partnership on Thursday to bring Gemini AI models to the former Agentforce 360 Platform.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.05%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.51%.

On trade, Treasury Secretary Bessent had reportedly said that President Donald Trump "is a go" for a meeting with Xi Jinping and that he was positive about the ongoing discussions with China. 

The U.S. government shutdown is now entering its third week. Prediction market Kalshi now sees the government shutdown lasting more than 39 days.

The Fed balance sheet is due later in the day. 

U.S. stocks ended mixed after a choppy session on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) gaining ground as upbeat earnings reports eclipsed U.S.-China trade tension worries.

data.title: Chip stocks mixed despite TSM's bullish views on AI megatrend; analysts largely positive
data.description:
Chip stocks were mixed after Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (NYSE:TSM) raised its outlook and highlighted "conviction in the AI megatrend."

The results and outlook also saw positive reactions from Wall Street analysts.

BofA raised the price target on TSM's stock to NT$1,800 ($360 for ADR) from NT$1,600 ($330 for ADR) and reiterated its Buy rating on the shares.

"Real demand from AI was one of the focal points during the earnings call, and we sense that the company is turning a bit more positive on the L-T growth trajectory (though keeping the mid-40s % CAGR). In our view, TSMC’s position in such an area remains solid by ensuring system-level performance via front/back-end manufacturing, while it begins to evaluate with “customers’ customers” to better fulfill demand for advanced technology. Eyeing on an unrivalled position amid the megatrend of AI growth, we re-it. Buy," said analysts led by Mike Yang.

Wells Fargo said TSM results and comments highlighted strengthening AI demand.

The analysts noted that TSM did not provide any specific update on its thoughts on graphics processing unit, or GPU, sales into China; only noting that it is confident in AI demand continuing to grow at a 40%+ compound annual growth rate, or CAGR, percentage over the next few years.

"We continue to consider TSMC's High Performance Computing, or HPC/AI comments to be viewed as positive for Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Broadcom (AVGO), and Marvell Technology (MRVL)," said analysts led by Aaron Rakers. "TSMC did not provide any quantified update on its CoWoS [Chip-on-Wafer-on-Substrate] capacity expansion plans - only noting that it is working "very hard to narrow the gap."

Earlier today, the global foundry reported third quarter results which beat analysts estimates. The company also raised its revenue growth forecast for the full year to the mid-30% range, compared to growth forecast of about 30% provided during the second quarter results. 

However, Chairman & CEO C.C. Wei warned about uncertainties and risk from the potential impact of tariff policies on the company's earnings call on Thursday.

"While we have not observed any change in our customers' behavior so far, we understand there are uncertainties and risk from the potential impact of tariff policies, especially in consumer-related and price-sensitive end market segment," said Wei. "As such, we will remain mindful of the potential impact and be prudent in our business planning going into 2026 by continuing to invest for the future megatrend."

Wei noted that companies such as TSM are using AI internally to drive greater productivity and efficiency. He noted that enterprise AI is another source of demand, and added that the company continues to see the rising emergence of sovereign AI.

"We are also happy to see continued strong outlook from our customers. In addition, we directly received very strong signals from our customers' customers, requesting the capacity to support their business. Thus, our conviction in the AI megatrend is strengthening and we believe the demand for semiconductor will continue to be very fundamental," said Wei.

Shares of TSM were largely flat but in the green. Intel (INTC), Marvell Technology (MRVL) and Analog Devices (ADI) rose about 1% each, while GlobalFoundries (GFS), Texas Instruments (TXN), and Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) were largely flat but in the green.

AI chipmakers, Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) rose nearly 1%. Broadcom (AVGO) jumped about 2%, while Qualcomm (QCOM) was largely flat but in the green.

Shares of chip equipment makers: ASML (ASML) rose about 2% and Applied Materials (AMAT) climbed nearly 1%. Lam Research (LRCX) fell around -1%, while KLA (KLAC) was flat but in the red.
